does_not_support: a single coherent variable-time stopping unitary
a physically retained coherent stopping-history register
free observation of exact output probabilities or garbage mass
a Top-k correctness or confidence certificate
generic off-grid correctness under finite QPE
a cleanup/resource theorem for coherent stopping-history replay
a new query-complexity upper bound or composition separation
a matching oracle lower bound
hardware evidence, quantum advantage, or a CCF-A publication claim
supports: sequential execution of tiny coherent unknown-boundary level unitaries
consecutive precision construction without an instance schedule input
exact per-level and cumulative canonical-oracle query reconciliation
hard-cap checks before every executed precision
durable direct-k membership-register and cleanup diagnostics
fail-closed output when the diagnostic predicate or budget fails

notes: All non-budget-ablation cases share one public error target and consecutive precision policy; no per-instance gap or phase schedule is supplied.
Means remain in the trusted fixture harness only and are used to construct the opaque canonical oracle and score outputs.
Each precision level executes the S2 coherent rank-copy-uncompute circuit and reconciles its canonical query ledger.
The adaptive stop decision reads exact statevector diagnostics in classical simulation; it is not a coherent variable-time controller or free observable.
MASK is diagnostic output with certificate withheld; ties, exhausted hard caps, and unaccepted levels fail closed as INCONCLUSIVE.
This panel supports implementation auditing only, not a new upper bound, matching lower bound, quantum advantage, or CCF-A claim.
